# s12: Configure minimal gix feature set for WASI compilation

- **Branch:** `pr1/s02-s11.s12/feat/wasi-gix-features`
- **Anchor:** `feat/wasi` (stacked on umbrella — created after tier-1 merges)
- **Deps:** s02 through s11 (all gating PRs)
- **Size:** M
- **Commit message:** `feat: configure minimal gix feature set for WASI compilation`

## Scope

- Audit all `gix` features used in the workspace
- `parallel` feature uses threading — disable for WASI
- `credentials` feature may shell out to git-credential — gate for WASI
- `sha1` may use platform-specific acceleration — check
- `zlib-rs` (gix dep) should be pure Rust — verify
- Configure minimal gix features in workspace Cargo.toml for WASI
- Fix any remaining std::thread or std::net usage in non-legacy paths

## Files

- `Cargo.toml` (workspace gix features)
- Various source files for remaining `#[cfg]` gates

## Acceptance Criteria

- `cargo check -p but --no-default-features --features wasi` gets significantly closer to compiling
- gix feature set documented in MEMORY.md

---

## Plan (generated by s12 planning agent)

### 1. Workspace `gix` entry (current state)

```toml
# Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]
gix = { version = "0.80.0", git = "https://github.com/GitoxideLabs/gitoxide", branch = "main",
        default-features = false, features = ["sha1"] }
```

`default-features = false` is already set. Only `sha1` is in the workspace baseline. Each crate
then adds features on top via `gix = { workspace = true, features = [...] }`.

---

### 2. Full feature matrix — all `gix` features requested across the workspace

| Feature | Requesting crates | WASI-safe? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sha1` | workspace default | Yes | Pure Rust SHA-1 via `sha1-checked`; no platform accel unless `max-performance` is also enabled |
| `serde` | `but-core` | Yes | Derive-only; no I/O |
| `status` | `but-core` | Yes | Drives `repo.status()` for worktree diffs; pure FS I/O |
| `dirwalk` | `but-core`, `but-fs`, `but-secret`, `gitbutler-project`, `gitbutler-oplog` | Yes | Filesystem directory walking via `gix-dir`; no threads |
| `merge` | `but-core`, `but-rebase`, `but-oxidize`, `gitbutler-repo`, `gitbutler-branch-actions` | Yes | `gix-merge` — pure algorithmic; uses `gix-command` for external merge drivers (not invoked automatically) |
| `revision` | `but-graph`, `but-rebase`, `but-meta(legacy)`, `gitbutler-branch-actions`, `but-core(dev)` | Yes | `gix-revision` — commit graph walking, describe; no threading |
| `worktree-mutation` | `but-workspace`, `but-api`, `gitbutler-tauri` | Conditional | `gix-worktree-state::checkout()` uses `gix-features` threading. However, `gix::worktree::state::checkout()` at rest (single-threaded fallback) should work if `parallel` is absent — needs verification |
| `blob-diff` | `gitbutler-branch-actions` (legacy) | Yes | `gix-diff` blob diffing; pure algorithm |
| `tree-editor` | `gitbutler-repo` (legacy) | Yes | In-memory tree editing; pure |
| `parallel` | `but-core`, `but-fs`, `but-secret`, `gitbutler-oplog` | **No** | Enables `gix-features` threading paths (crossbeam-channel). WASI has no threads in wasip2 without `wasi-threads` proposal |
| `credentials` | `but-core`, `but-fs`, `but-secret`, `gitbutler-project`, `gitbutler-oplog` | **No** | `gix-credentials` shells out to `git credential` via `gix-command`; process::Command not available in WASI. Used actively in `but-secret/src/secret.rs` |
| `blocking-http-transport-reqwest-rust-tls` | `gitbutler-project` (legacy) | **No** | HTTP network client; WASI has no sockets |
| `max-performance` | `gitbutler-tauri`, `gitbutler-cli` (legacy) | **No** | Pulls in platform-specific SHA acceleration and threading |
| `tracing` | `but` (binary) | Yes | Hooks `gix-trace` into `tracing` crate; no I/O |
| `tracing-detail` | `but` (binary) | Yes | More verbose tracing spans; no I/O |

---

### 3. Feature analysis: WASI-incompatible features

#### `parallel`
- Enables multi-threaded code paths in `gix-features` via `crossbeam-channel`.
- `gix-odb`, `gix-pack`, `gix-status` all use the `parallel` infrastructure from `gix-features`.
- WASI (wasip2 without `wasi-threads`) has no thread support.
- **Impact:** `but-core`, `but-fs`, `but-secret` all request this. None of these are in the mandatory
  non-legacy `but` path for the `wasi` feature flag — they are all gated by `legacy = true` or
  are not in the direct transitive chain of `but` with `--no-default-features --features wasi`.
- **Verdict:** Drop from workspace default. Gate with `#[cfg(not(target_family = "wasm"))]` or
  a `non-wasi` Cargo feature in each requesting crate.

#### `credentials`
- `gix-credentials` uses `gix-command` (process::Command) to shell out to `git credential` helpers.
- `gix-command` uses `std::process::Command` — unavailable in WASI.
- `but-secret/src/secret.rs` directly uses `gix::credentials::helper::{Cascade, Action, NextAction}`.
- `but-secret` is a mandatory (non-optional) dependency of the `but` binary.
- **Impact:** This is a hard blocker for the `but` crate compiling to WASI.
- **Verdict:** Gate `but-secret`'s credentials code behind `#[cfg(not(target_family = "wasm"))]`.
  The `credentials` feature should be removed from `but-secret`'s gix dependency for WASI. Requires
  either a `wasi` feature on `but-secret` that disables the git_credentials module, or runtime
  `cfg` gating of the relevant types. Since `but-secret` already has no `wasi` feature today,
  this needs a new feature flag.

#### `blocking-http-transport-reqwest-rust-tls`
- Only in `gitbutler-project` — a legacy crate that is optional behind the `legacy` feature of `but`.
- Not a blocker for `--no-default-features --features wasi` on `but`.

#### `max-performance`
- Only in `gitbutler-tauri` and `gitbutler-cli` — not in the `but` binary's WASI path.
- Not a blocker.

---

### 4. Feature analysis: WASI-safe features

#### `sha1`
- Uses `sha1-checked` crate — pure Rust. No platform acceleration unless `max-performance` is also
  present. WASI-safe.

#### `zlib-rs` (implicit via `gix-features`)
- `gix-features` in the Cargo.lock already pulls in `zlib-rs` (pure Rust). This is the replacement
  for C libz. WASI-safe.

#### `status`, `dirwalk`
- `gix-status` depends on: `gix-diff`, `gix-dir`, `gix-features`, `gix-filter`, `gix-fs`, `gix-index`,
  `gix-worktree`. All filesystem-based; no threading required per se (threading is optional in
  `gix-features`).
- `gix-dir` (dirwalk) has no threading dependency in its lock entry.
- Both are needed by `but-core::diff::worktree_changes()` which is a core capability.
- **Verdict:** WASI-safe.

#### `merge`
- `gix-merge` uses `gix-command` for external merge drivers, but only when explicitly invoked.
  The common code paths (blob merge, tree merge) are pure Rust algorithms.
- `gix-merge` is in the dependency graph of the non-legacy crate chain.
- **Verdict:** Conditional — safe as long as external merge driver invocation is not triggered.
  This is acceptable since WASI operations would be read-only or use built-in algorithms.

#### `revision`
- `gix-revision`: commit ancestry walking, describe functionality, rev-spec parsing. All pure Rust.
- **Verdict:** WASI-safe.

#### `worktree-mutation`
- `gix::worktree::state::checkout()` is called by `but-workspace` (non-legacy code):
  - `crates/but-workspace/src/tree_manipulation/discard_worktree_changes.rs:367`
  - `crates/but-workspace/src/tree_manipulation/discard_worktree_changes.rs:446`
- `gix-worktree-state` (lock entry) depends on `gix-features` but not specifically on threading.
  In gix, the `parallel` feature in `gix-features` enables a multi-threaded checkout; without it,
  checkout falls back to sequential. The `worktree-mutation` feature itself does not require
  `parallel`.
- **Verdict:** WASI-safe (requires sequential checkout path, which is the fallback when `parallel`
  is absent).

#### `tracing`, `tracing-detail`
- Pure instrumentation via `gix-trace`. No I/O or platform code.
- **Verdict:** WASI-safe.

#### `serde`
- Enables `#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]` on gix types. No I/O.
- **Verdict:** WASI-safe.

---

### 5. `gix::interrupt::IS_INTERRUPTED` — signal handling analysis

`IS_INTERRUPTED` is used in three places:

1. `crates/but-workspace/src/tree_manipulation/discard_worktree_changes.rs:373`
2. `crates/but-workspace/src/tree_manipulation/discard_worktree_changes.rs:452`
3. `crates/but-core/src/diff/worktree.rs:818`

In gix's design, `gix::interrupt::IS_INTERRUPTED` is a `static AtomicBool` — it is just a shared
flag that can be polled. It is NOT a signal handler registration. The signal handler is set up
separately when the `gix::interrupt` module's signal integration is enabled.

The static `IS_INTERRUPTED: AtomicBool` itself is WASI-safe — atomic types work in WASI.
The signal-handler registration (e.g., `gix::interrupt::init_handler()`) would NOT be WASI-safe,
but there is no evidence that it is called anywhere in the codebase. The code only reads from the
flag. **This is not a blocker.**

---

### 6. What `but` (WASI path) actually needs from gix

The WASI-relevant dependency chain for `but` with `--no-default-features --features wasi` is:

```
but
  ├── but-core          → gix: status, dirwalk, serde, merge (IS_INTERRUPTED as AtomicBool)
  ├── but-graph         → gix: revision
  ├── but-rebase        → gix: revision, merge
  ├── but-workspace     → gix: worktree-mutation
  ├── but-oxidize       → gix: merge
  ├── but-ctx           → gix: (workspace default only — sha1)
  ├── but-hunk-assign.  → gix: (workspace default only)
  ├── but-hunk-dep.     → gix: (workspace default only, features = [])
  ├── but-serde         → gix: (workspace default only)
  ├── but-project-handle→ gix: (workspace default only)
  ├── but-api           → gix: worktree-mutation
  ├── but-link          → gix: (workspace default only)
  └── but (binary)      → gix: tracing, tracing-detail
```

**Problematic transitive paths (via `but-secret`):**
- `but` → `but-secret` → `gix: dirwalk, credentials, parallel`
- `but-secret` directly uses `gix::credentials::helper::*`

---

### 7. Proposed minimal WASI-safe gix feature set

**Workspace default (`Cargo.toml`):**
```toml
gix = { version = "0.80.0", git = "https://github.com/GitoxideLabs/gitoxide", branch = "main",
        default-features = false,
        features = ["sha1"] }
```
No change to the workspace baseline — it already has only `sha1`.

**Per-crate changes needed for WASI compilation:**

| Crate | Current features | Proposed WASI-safe features | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| `but-core` | `dirwalk, credentials, parallel, serde, status, merge` | `dirwalk, serde, status, merge` | Remove `credentials`, `parallel` |
| `but-secret` | `dirwalk, credentials, parallel` | Add `wasi` feature that drops `credentials` usage | Gate `git_credentials` module behind `#[cfg]` |
| `but-fs` | `dirwalk, credentials, parallel` | `dirwalk` (legacy only — but-fs not in WASI path) | Entire crate is legacy |
| `gitbutler-oplog` | `dirwalk, credentials, parallel` | N/A — legacy, excluded from WASI build | No change needed |
| `gitbutler-project` | `dirwalk, credentials, parallel, blocking-http-transport-reqwest-rust-tls` | N/A — legacy | No change needed |
| `but` (binary) | `tracing, tracing-detail` | `tracing, tracing-detail` | No change |
| `but-rebase` | `revision, merge` | `revision, merge` | No change |
| `but-graph` | `revision` | `revision` | No change |
| `but-workspace` | `worktree-mutation` | `worktree-mutation` | No change |
| `but-oxidize` | `merge` | `merge` | No change |
| `but-api` | `worktree-mutation` | `worktree-mutation` | No change |

**Effective union of WASI-safe features on `gix`:**
`sha1`, `serde`, `status`, `dirwalk`, `merge`, `revision`, `worktree-mutation`, `tracing`, `tracing-detail`

---

### 8. Key code changes required

1. **`crates/but-core/Cargo.toml`:** Remove `credentials` and `parallel` from gix features.
   - The `credentials` feature is not referenced in `but-core/src/` code (confirmed by search).
   - `parallel` is not directly referenced either — it was likely inherited for performance.

2. **`crates/but-secret/Cargo.toml` + `src/secret.rs`:**
   - Add a `wasi` feature to `but-secret`.
   - Gate the `git_credentials` module behind `#[cfg(not(target_family = "wasm"))]`.
   - The `gix` dependency in `but-secret` should drop `credentials` and `parallel` when `wasi` is active.
   - This is the **highest priority fix** — `but-secret` is a mandatory non-optional dependency
     of `but`.

3. **`crates/but/Cargo.toml`:**
   - Add `but-secret/wasi` to the `wasi` feature definition:
     ```toml
     wasi = ["but-secret/wasi"]
     ```

4. **`crates/but-core/src/diff/worktree.rs:818`** — `gix::interrupt::IS_INTERRUPTED`:
   - No change required. `AtomicBool` is WASI-safe.

---

### 9. Open questions / blockers

None blocking for plan. Potential runtime issues to verify:

- **Sequential checkout:** `gix::worktree::state::checkout()` without `parallel` falls back to
  sequential iteration. Verify that `gix-worktree-state` compiles cleanly without `parallel` in
  `gix-features`. This should work by design but needs a test compile.

- **`gix-merge` and `gix-command`:** `gix-merge` depends on `gix-command` for external merge drivers.
  `gix-command` uses `std::process::Command`. Even though it won't be called at runtime, it will
  be compiled in. This may cause linker or compile errors on WASI if `std::process::Command` is
  not available. Check whether `merge` feature pull of `gix-command` into compilation unit causes
  issues under `wasm32-wasip2`. This is the concern that may require further gating.

- **`gix-credentials` compile-time:** Even if we remove the `credentials` gix feature from
  `but-core`, `gix-credentials` is still compiled as part of the `gix` crate itself (it's in the
  resolved `gix` lock entry). The `credentials` *feature* gates which APIs are re-exported from
  `gix::credentials`, but the `gix-credentials` sub-crate may still be in the dependency tree
  regardless. Verify that disabling the `credentials` feature prevents `gix-command` from being
  a hard transitive dependency (or that `gix-command` compiles on WASI anyway).
